title: jeng-92-Liquid_rocket_spray_combustion_stability_analysis
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
1.  Mathematical equations:
    *   E = \[pub, (pu^2 + p)b, puvb, (pe+p)ub]^T
    *   F = \[pvb, puvb, (pv^2 + p)b, (pe+p)vb]^T
    *   H = H1 + H2
    *   H1 = \[0, p*ub^2, 0, 0]^T
    *   H2= \[\Sigma wi, \Sigma[wivi + Dxi], \Sigma[wiupi + Dyi], \Sigma[wi(Delta qi + xi^2/2) +bSigma[uDxi + vDyi]]^T
    *   p=rho*RT = (gamma -1)[e-rho*q^2/2]
    *   Tm^-1.M^-1.A.M.Tx = Ax
    *   Tm^-1.M^-1.B.M.Ty = Ay
    *   A = MT(A+ + A-)TM^-1 = A+ + A-
    *   B = MT(A+ + A-)TM^-1 = B+ + B-
    *   |A| = MT(A - A-)TM^-1 = A+ - A-
    *   |B| = MT(A - A-)TM^-1 = B+ - B-
    *   dQjk/dt = -R(Qj,k)

2.  Graphs/charts: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  Tables: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  Diagrams: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  Key numbers, constants, parameters:
    *gamma
    *u, u+c, u-c eigenvalues of Jacobian
    *v, v+c, v-c eigenvalues of Jacobian
    *c = sqrt((gamma*p)/rho)
    *A = dE/dQ
    *B = dF/dQ
    *Q=rho*q^2/2

title: jeng-92-Liquid_rocket_spray_combustion_stability_analysis
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**
    *   du_p/dt = (3*rho_g*C_D)/(4*rho_p*d_p) * |u_g - u_p| * (u_g - u_p)
    * d*x_p/dt = u_p
    *   u_p approx u_po * exp(-Delta t / tau) + u_g * {1 - exp(-Delta t / tau)}
    *   x_p approx x_po + u_p * Delta t
    *   m_dot = (pi/4) * rho_p * d_p * B * K_v * K_p
    *   K_v = 1 + 0.3 * Re^(1/2) * Pr^(1/3)
    *   m_dot' / m_dot = alpha * (p'/p)
    * K_p = 1 + alpha * (p'(t;x_p) / p(x_p))

2.  **Graphs/charts:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:**
   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**
    *   n (power): used in the proportionality of pressure to the mass burning rate
    * alpha: combustions response index
    * d_p: parcel
    * u_p: parcel velocity
    * x_p: parcel position
    *Re: reynolds number
    *Pr: prandtl number

title: jeng-92-Liquid_rocket_spray_combustion_stability_analysis
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   Equations (26) - and (29)

**2. Graphs/Charts**

*   **Figure 3:** mass flow rate, Mach number, and temperature distributions
*   **Figure 4:** stable combustion behavior is demonstrated for a spray length to combustor length ratio of 0.6 and a combustion response index of 0.9.
*   **Figure 5:** shows intrinsic instability when the combustion response index is increased to 1.0- classical exponential growth of the pressure wave in the linear regime
*   **Figure 7**: fluctuations in velocity, temperature, and entropy are shown in relation to fluctuations in pressure during unstable combustion
*    **Figure 8**: pressure profile
*     **Figure 9:** stability limit is plotted against normalized combustion response strength and normalized mean spray length.

**3. Tables**

*   **Table 2:** Propellant properties
*   **Table 3:** Combustor physical conditions
*   **Table 4:** Burning rate constants and resulting mean spray lengths

**4. Diagrams**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Spray length to combustor length ratio of 0.6
*   Combustion response index of 0.9
*   Combustion response index increased to 1.0
*   1D grid contained 101 evenly spaced nodes
*   3% of total spray

title: jeng-92-Liquid_rocket_spray_combustion_stability_analysis
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 9]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   "combustion response is linearly related to pressure as seen in Eq. (28)" (Equation 28 is not provided)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Figure 10: Stability behavior for pulsed and non-pulsed cases.
*   Figure 11: Spectral composition of the waveform.
*   Figure 12: Pressure histories (spray length to combustor length ratio of 0.6, combustion response index of 0.1)
*   Figure 13: Exploded view of short term pressure histories with waveforms at theta = 0 and theta = pi exactly half a period out of phase.
*   Figure 14: Superimposed high frequency components (low level noise and possible precursors to mode switching.)
*   Figure 15: Long term instability mode (time interval of 35 to 40 msec), the pressure at theta = pi/2 oscillating at the fundamental frequency with very high amplitude peaks centered between the lower amplitude ones manifested at theta = 0 and theta = pi.
*   Figures 16 and 17: Flooded contour plots (combustor pressure history during short and long term modes.)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   6th order central differencing
*   2D grid: 51 nodes (axial coordinate), 30 nodes (tangential coordinate)
*   Spray length to combustor length ratio: 0.6
*   Combustion response index: 0.1
*   Phase angle: pi/2 (between tangential locations of pressure recordings)
*   Mode switching occurs during: 10 to 25 msec
*   Short term instability: up to 10 msec
*   Long term instability: 35 to 40 msec
*   Experimentally observed period: 470 µs
*   Calculated period for short term instability: 435 µs
*   Research combustor diameter: 11 inches
*   Analysis combustor diameter: 10 inches

*   tau = (4/3) (rho_p / rho_g) (dp / CD) (1 / |u_g - u_p|)

    | Scheme               | κ    |

    | 2nd Order Upwind     | -1   |
    | 2nd Order Central    | 1    |
    | 3rd Order Biased Upwind | 1/3 |

*   **Table 2: Propellant Properties**

    |          | Value       | Units             |
    | :------- | :---------- | :---------------- |
    | γ        | 1.2         |                   |
    | Cp       | 1800        | J/kg K            |
    | Δq       | 20(10)^6    | J/kg              |
    | ρ        | 750         | kg/m^3            |
    | μ        | 5(10)^-6  | kg/m s            |
    | λ        | 1(10)^-4   | W/m K             |

*   **Table 3: Combustor Physical Conditions**

    |        | Value | Units   |
    | :----- | :---- | :------ |
    | l1     | 0.5   | m       |
    | Ac     | 0.05  | m^2     |
    | Pc     | 34    | atm     |
    | m_dot  | 40    | kg/s    |
    | m_dot/m_dot | 0.25  |         |
    | do     | 100   | μm      |
    | u1     | 75    | m/s     |

*   **Table 4: Mean Burning Rate / Spray Length**

    | β   | lr/l1 |
    | :---- | :---- |
    | 1.4 | 0.36 |
    | 1.0 | 0.60 |
    | 0.8 | 0.90 |
**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1: Schematic of physical combustor and equivalent annular computational geometry.**
        *   Axisymmetric Combustor
        *   Equivalent Annular Combustor
        *   Components: b(x), Propellant Injection, Nozzle Entrance, Lc, Y=RB
*   **Figure 2: Schematic of rectangular uniform pdf in relation to computational grid.**
        *   Computational Grid: Grid points  (j-1,k+1), <jk+1), (j+1,k+1)
        *   Grid points (j-1,k), (jk), (j+1,k)
        * Grid points (j-1,k-1), (jk-1), (j+1,k-1)
        *   Other: Vp, (Xp,Yp), Uinj, Δtinj, H=ΔY, W = Uinj Δtinj
        * Axis x, y

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   **Scheme Switching Parameter:** κ
*   **Propellant Properties:** γ, Cp, Δq, ρ, μ, λ
*   **Combustor Physical Conditions:** l1, Ac, Pc, m_dot, m_dot/m_dot, do, u1

text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None explicit. Implicitly plots relate pressure (P), velocity (u), temperature (T) and entropy (s) fluctuation.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 7: Fluctuation of flow parameters during unstable combustion.**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Reduced Acoustic Fluctuation
        *   X-axis: Time (msec)
    *   Curves:
        *   Pressure: P'/P (dashed line)
        *   Velocity: u'/U (solid line).
        *   Temperature: T'/T (dashed line)
        *   Reduced Entropy: s'/Cv (solid line)
    *   X-axis values range from 100.0 to 105.0

*   **Figure 8: Pressure profiles during unstable combustion.**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: P/Pref
        *   X-axis: x/l0
    *   Curves:
        *   Parameterized by time (t) in msec.
        *   t = 96.1 msec, t = 95.9 msec, t = 96.3 msec, t = 96.5 msec, t = 95.7 msec
    *   Constants: P34 atm, l = 0.5 m,  β = 1.0 mm²/s, α = 1.0
    *   X-axis values range from 0.00 to 1.00
    *   Y-axis values range from 0.60 to 1.20

*   **Figure 9: Longitudinal-mode combustor stability map.**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: αβ/βref (dimensionless)
        *   X-axis: l/l0 (dimensionless)
    *   Curves:
        *   Boundary between "Unstable" and "Stable" regions based on the position of data points.
    *   Constant: βw = 1.0 mm²/s

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Pressure: P34 atm
*   Length Scale: l= 0.5 m
*   Diffusivity: β = 1.0 mm²/s, βw = 1.0 mm²/s
*   Parameter α = 1.0
*   Time: t = 96.1 msec, t = 95.9 msec, t = 96.3 msec, t = 96.5 msec, t = 95.7 msec
